title: How to Order Döner in Berlin - Quick Summary
items: **The Berlin specialty is the Gemüse Kebap: meat and salad plus a scoop of fried vegetables, fresh mint, lemon and crumbled white cheese.** A plain meat döner exists too, but the Gemüse version is the local signature.

**Pick your form first.** Döner im Brot (folded flatbread, the default), Dürüm (rolled wrap), Dönerteller (plate with fries, no bread), or Döner-Box (meat and fries). If it is your first one, get a Dürüm.
**One sentence does it: "Einmal [form] mit [meat], mit alles, mit [sauce], bitte."** "Einmal" means one and dodges the German article guessing game, so it works for every form.

**Key words: mit alles = everything (salad and onions); ohne Zwiebeln = no onions.** Sauces are usually Knoblauch (garlic-yogurt), Kräuter (herb) and scharf (chili). "Einen Döner, bitte" on its own still gets you a good one.
**Price in 2026: about 7 to 8 euros,** up from 3 to 4 before the pandemic, a jump Germans nicknamed "Dönerflation." Most kiosks take cash and many now take cards.
**You do not need to queue for an hour at Mustafa's.** Busy local kiosks a few streets over do the same job with no wait. For no meat, order "vegetarisch" or ask for Falafel im Brot.
